text:
Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tommy who dreamed of travelling the world. He loved history, exploring ancient ruins, and imagining himself exploring lands far beyond his hometown.

One summer, Tommy got his chance. His grandfather, noticing how much the young boy longed for adventure, gifted him an atlas filled with maps of countries around the globe. With his new treasure, Tommy spent every free moment poring over the maps.

Tommy dreamt of being an archaeologist. He imagined ancient civilizations and their secrets. He studied history, studied maps, and one rainy afternoon, he decided to build a model of a Greek city-state.

Weeks turned into months. Tommy meticulously constructed every building, decorated with leaves and pottery shards found around his neighborhood. But his work was too detailed – it seemed almost real. He even set up pretend parties for philosophers and athletes.

His grand unveiling in the backyard received an explosion of wonder and applause. Tommy felt incredibly proud of his creation. His neighbors
